records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/359] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hajjal from Abi Ishaq Tha’laba from Zurara b. A’yan from One of the two عليهما السلام who said: Allah has not been worshipped by something like Bada’
records.arabicText: [359/1] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد بن عيسى، عن الحجال، عن أبي إسحاق ثعلبة، عن زرارة بن أعين، عن أحدهما عليهما السلام قال: ما عبد الله بشئ مثل البداء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/359] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hajjal from Abi Ishaq Tha’laba from Zurara b. A’yan from One of the two عليهما السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah has not been worshipped by something like Bada’

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/360] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim and Hafs b. al-Bakhtari and others apart from them both from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام that he (the Imam) said concerning the verse: “Allah erases what He wills and establishes” (13:39) - Does He erase other than that which was established (before)? And does He establish other than that which was not there (at all)?
records.arabicText: [360/2] الكافي: علي بن إبراهيم، عن أبيه، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن هشام بن سالم وحفص بن البختري وغيرهما، عن أبى عبدالله عليه السلام قال في هذه الآية: يَمْحُوا اللَّهُ مَا يَشَاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ قال: فقال: وهل يمحى إلا ما كان ثابتا؟ وهل يثبت إلا ما لم يكن؟
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/360] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim and Hafs b. al-Bakhtari and others apart from them both from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام that he (the Imam) said concerning the verse: “Allah erases what He wills and establishes” (13:39) -
records.thaqalaynMatn: Does He erase other than that which was established (before)? And does He establish other than that which was not there (at all)?

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/361] al-Kafi: Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: Allah did not appoint a prophet unless He takes from him (a commitment to) three properties: Affirming worship as due only to Him, renouncing the rivals (idols set up besides Allah), and that Allah brings forward what He wills and puts back what He wills.
records.arabicText: [361/3] الكافي: علي، عن أبيه، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن هشام بن سالم، عن محمد بن مسلم، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: ما بعث الله نبيا حتى ياخذ عليه ثلاث خصال: الاقرار له بالعبودية، وخلع الانداد، وأن الله يقدم ما يشاء ويؤخر ما يشاء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/361] al-Kafi: Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah did not appoint a prophet unless He takes from him (a commitment to) three properties: Affirming worship as due only to Him, renouncing the rivals (idols set up besides Allah), and that Allah brings forward what He wills and puts back what He wills.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/362] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara from Humram from Abi Ja’far. He said: I asked him about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “He decreed a term and the appointed term is with Him” (6:2). He said: There are two terms. A term that is final and another term which is deferred.
records.arabicText: [362/4] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن ابن فضال، عن ابن بكير، عن زرارة عن حمران، عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: سألته عن قول الله عز وجل: قَضَى أَجَلاً وَأَجَلٌ مُّسمًّى عِندَهُ قال: هما أجلان: أجل محتوم وأجل موقوف
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/362] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara from Humram from Abi Ja’far. He said: I asked him about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “He decreed a term and the appointed term is with Him” (6:2).
records.thaqalaynMatn: He said: There are two terms. A term that is final and another term which is deferred.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 5. [5/363] al-Kafi: A number of our companions from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Abi Umayr from Ja’far b. Uthman from Sama’a from Abi Basir (and Wuhayb b. Hafs from Abi Basir) from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: Allah has two kinds of knowledge. A knowledge which is hidden and stored-up. No one knows it but Him. From that does Bada’ happen. And a knowledge which He taught His angels, messengers and prophets. So we too do know it.
records.arabicText: [363/5] الكافي: عدة من أصحابنا، عن أحمد بن محمد بن عيسى، عن ابن ابي عمير، عن جعفر ابن عثمان، عن سماعة، عن ابي بصير، ووهيب بن حفص، عن ابي بصير، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: إن لله علمين: علم مكنون مخزون، لا يعلمه إلا هو، من ذلك يكون البداء وعلم علمه ملائكته ورسله وأنبياءه فنحن نعلمه
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5. [5/363] al-Kafi: A number of our companions from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Abi Umayr from Ja’far b. Uthman from Sama’a from Abi Basir (and Wuhayb b. Hafs from Abi Basir) from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah has two kinds of knowledge. A knowledge which is hidden and stored-up. No one knows it but Him. From that does Bada’ happen. And a knowledge which He taught His angels, messengers and prophets. So we too do know it.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 6. [6/364] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Husayn b. Sa’id from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: Nothing becomes apparent to Allah concerning something (to make Him change His decree) except that it was in His knowledge before it appears to Him (to change it).
records.arabicText: [364/6] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن الحسين بن سعيد، عن الحسن بن محبوب، عن عبدالله بن سنان، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: ما بدا لله في شئ إلا كان في علمه قبل أن يبدو له
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. [6/364] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Husayn b. Sa’id from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Nothing becomes apparent to Allah concerning something (to make Him change His decree) except that it was in His knowledge before it appears to Him (to change it).

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 7. [7/365] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from al-Rayyan b. al-Salt who said: I heard al-Ridha عليه السلام: Allah has never appointed a prophet except with the prohibition of wine and that he (the prophet) accept from Allah the Bada’
records.arabicText: [365/7] الكافي: علي بن إبراهيم، عن أبيه، عن الريان بن الصلت قال: سمعت الرضا عليه السلام يقول: ما بعث الله نبيا قط إلا بتحريم الخمر وأن يقر لله بالبداء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 7. [7/365] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from al-Rayyan b. al-Salt who said: I heard al-Ridha عليه السلام:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah has never appointed a prophet except with the prohibition of wine and that he (the prophet) accept from Allah the Bada’

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 8. [8/366] al-Kafi: Ali b. Muhammad b. Abdallah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from more than one from Ali b. Asbat from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: Abu al-Hasan عليه السلام said: Abu Ja’farعليه السلام said: A man from the Children of Israel had a son whom he used to love very much. He had a vision in his sleep and it was said to him: ‘Your son will die the very night that he enters upon his wife (i.e. on the wedding night)’. He (the Imam) said: when it was that night, and he consummated with her, the father believed that will happen, but he woke up finding his son well. His father came to him and said: O my son did you do anything of the good yesterday? He said: No. Except that a beggar came to the door and they had kept preserved for me some food and I gave it to the beggar. He (the father) said: Through this did Allah avert it (death) from you.
records.arabicText: [366/8] الكافي: علي بن محمد بن عبدالله، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن غير واحد، عن علي بن أسباط، عن الحسن بن الجهم قال: قال أبو الحسن عليه السلام: قال أبوجعفر عليه السلام: إن رجلا من بني إسرائيل كان له ابن وكان له محبا فاتي في منامه فقيل له: إن ابنك ليلة يدخل بأهله يموت، قال: فلما كان تلك الليلة وبنى عليه أبوه توقع أبوه ذلك فأصبح ابنه سليما فأتاه أبوه فقال له: يا بني هل عملت البارحة شيئا من الخير؟ قال: لا إلا أن سائلا أتى الباب وقد كانوا ادخروا لي طعاما فأعطيته السائل، فقال: بهذا دفع الله عنك
records.thaqalaynSanad: 8. [8/366] al-Kafi: Ali b. Muhammad b. Abdallah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from more than one from Ali b. Asbat from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: Abu al-Hasan عليه السلام said: Abu Ja’farعليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: A man from the Children of Israel had a son whom he used to love very much. He had a vision in his sleep and it was said to him: ‘Your son will die the very night that he enters upon his wife (i.e. on the wedding night)’. He (the Imam) said: when it was that night, and he consummated with her, the father believed that will happen, but he woke up finding his son well. His father came to him and said: O my son did you do anything of the good yesterday? He said: No. Except that a beggar came to the door and they had kept preserved for me some food and I gave it to the beggar. He (the father) said: Through this did Allah avert it (death) from you.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 9. [9/367] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ayyub b. Nuh from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: Allah Mighty and Majestic has not been magnified with the like of Bada’
records.arabicText: [367/9] التوحيد: ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن أيوب بن نوح، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن هشام بن سالم، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما عظم الله عز وجل بمثل البداء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 9. [9/367] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ayyub b. Nuh from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah Mighty and Majestic has not been magnified with the like of Bada’

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 10. [10/368] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Hammad b. Isa from Rib’i b. Abdallah from al-Fudhayl b. Yasar who said: I heard Aba Ja’far عليه السلام saying: Knowledge is of two kinds. A knowledge with Allah which is hidden. No one has seen into it among his creation. And a knowledge which He taught His angels and messengers. So as for that which He taught His angels and messengers then it must occur. He does not belie Himself, nor His angels, nor His messengers. As for the hidden knowledge with Him then He brings forward from it what He wills and puts back from it what He wills, and He establishes what He wills.
records.arabicText: [368/10] الكافي: محمد بن إسماعيل، عن الفضل بن شاذان، عن حماد بن عيسى، عن ربعي بن عبدالله، عن الفضيل بن يسار قال: سمعت أبا جعفر عليه السلام يقول: العلم علمان: فعلم عند الله مخزون لم يطلع عليه أحدا من خلقه وعلم علمه وملائكته ورسله، فما علمه ملائكته ورسله فإنه سيكون، لا يكذب نفسه ولا ملائكته ولا رسله، وعلم عنده مخزون يقدم منه ما يشاء، ويؤخر منه ما يشاء، ويثبت ما يشاء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 10. [10/368] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Hammad b. Isa from Rib’i b. Abdallah from al-Fudhayl b. Yasar who said: I heard Aba Ja’far عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Knowledge is of two kinds. A knowledge with Allah which is hidden. No one has seen into it among his creation. And a knowledge which He taught His angels and messengers. So as for that which He taught His angels and messengers then it must occur. He does not belie Himself, nor His angels, nor His messengers. As for the hidden knowledge with Him then He brings forward from it what He wills and puts back from it what He wills, and He establishes what He wills.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 11. [11/369] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Hammad from Rib’i from al-Fudhayl who said: I heard Aba Ja’far عليه السلام saying: From the matters are matters which are deferred with Allah. He brings forward from them what He wills and puts back from them what He wills.
records.arabicText: [369/11] الكافي: محمد بن إسماعيل، عن الفضل بن شاذان، عن حماد، عن ربعي، عن الفضيل قال: سمعت أبا جعفر عليه السلام يقول: من الامور امور موقوفة عند الله يقدم منها ما يشاء ويؤخر منها ما يشاء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 11. [11/369] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Hammad from Rib’i from al-Fudhayl who said: I heard Aba Ja’far عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: From the matters are matters which are deferred with Allah. He brings forward from them what He wills and puts back from them what He wills.

records.chapter: Bada’
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 12. [12/370] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Mansur b. Hazim who said: I asked Aba Abdillah عليه السلام - Does there happen something today that was not in the knowledge of Allah yesterday? He said: No. May the one who says this be abased by Allah! I said: Consider - whatever has happened and whatever will happen up to the Day of Judgment is it not in the knowledge of Allah? He said: Yes. Before He created the creation.
records.arabicText: [370/12] الكافي: علي بن إبراهيم، عن محمد بن عيسى، عن يونس، عن منصور بن حازم قال: سألت أبا عبدالله عليه السلام هل يكون اليوم شئ لم يكن في علم الله بالامس؟ قال: لا، من قال هذا فأخزاه الله، قلت: أرأيت ما كان وما هو كائن إلى يوم القيامة أليس في علم الله؟ قال: بلى قبل أن يخلق الخلق
records.thaqalaynSanad: 12. [12/370] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Mansur b. Hazim who said: I asked Aba Abdillah عليه السلام -
records.thaqalaynMatn: Does there happen something today that was not in the knowledge of Allah yesterday? He said: No. May the one who says this be abased by Allah! I said: Consider - whatever has happened and whatever will happen up to the Day of Judgment is it not in the knowledge of Allah? He said: Yes. Before He created the creation.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/371] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim who said: Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said: O Muhammad, people keep being conversed with unless they begin talking about Allah, so when you hear that then say: ‘there is no god but Allah, the Unique, the one whom there is nothing like Him’
records.arabicText: [371/1] الكافي: علي بن إبراهيم، عن أبيه، عن ابن ابي عمير، عن أبي أيوب، عن محمد بن مسلم قال: قال أبوعبد الله عليه السلام: يا محمد إن الناس لا يزال بهم المنطق حتى يتكلموا في الله فإذا سمعتم ذلك فقولوا: لا إله إلا الله الواحد الذي ليس كمثله شئ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/371] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim who said: Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: O Muhammad, people keep being conversed with unless they begin talking about Allah, so when you hear that then say: ‘there is no god but Allah, the Unique, the one whom there is nothing like Him’

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/372] al-Kafi: A number of our companions from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Khalid from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Muhammad b. Humran from Abi Ubayda al-Hadhdha who said: Abu Ja’far عليه السلام said: O Ziyad - beware of argumentation because it creates doubt, nullifies deeds and degrades its initiator, and it may happen that a man speaks something for which he is never forgiven. There was in days gone by a people who abandoned studying what they were entrusted and sought the knowledge of what they were asked to refrain from, until their talks turned to Allah and they became confused, so much so that a man would be called from in front of him but he would answer the one who is behind him and he would be called from behind and would answer the one in front.
records.arabicText: [372/2] الكافي: عدة من أصحابنا، عن أحمد بن محمد بن خالد، عن أبيه، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن محمد بن حمران، عن أبي عبيدة الحذاء قال: قال أبوجعفر عليه السلام: يا زياد إياك والخصومات فإنها تورث الشك وتهبط العمل وتردي صاحبها وعسى أن يتكلم بالشئ فلا يغفر له إنه كان فيما مضى قوم تركوا علم ما وكلوا به وطلبوا علم ما كفوه حتى انتهى كلامهم إلى الله فتحيروا حتى ان كان الرجل ليدعي من بين يديه فيجيب من خلفه ويدعي من خلفه فيجيب من بين يديه
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/372] al-Kafi: A number of our companions from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Khalid from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Muhammad b. Humran from Abi Ubayda al-Hadhdha who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Ja’far عليه السلام said: O Ziyad - beware of argumentation because it creates doubt, nullifies deeds and degrades its initiator, and it may happen that a man speaks something for which he is never forgiven. There was in days gone by a people who abandoned studying what they were entrusted and sought the knowledge of what they were asked to refrain from, until their talks turned to Allah and they became confused, so much so that a man would be called from in front of him but he would answer the one who is behind him and he would be called from behind and would answer the one in front.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/373] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Abi Umayr from Abd al-Rahman b. al-Hajjaj from Sulayman b. Khalid who said: Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said: Allah Mighty and Majestic says: “and to your Lord is the End (finality)” (53:42) so if your talks reach to Allah then remain silent.
records.arabicText: [373/3] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن عبدالرحمن بن الحجاج، عن سليمان بن خالد قال: قال أبوعبدالله عليه السلام: إن الله عزوجل يقول: وَأَنَّ إِلَى رَبِّكَ الْمُنتَهَى فإذا انتهى الكلام إلى الله فأمسكوا
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/373] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Abi Umayr from Abd al-Rahman b. al-Hajjaj from Sulayman b. Khalid who said: Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah Mighty and Majestic says: “and to your Lord is the End (finality)” (53:42) so if your talks reach to Allah then remain silent.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/374] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara b. A’yan from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: There was a king of great status who was in a council of his when he touched upon (started discussing) the Lord Blessed and Elevated. Suddenly he disappeared and it was not known where he was (could not be found).
records.arabicText: [374/4] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد بن عيسى، عن ابن فضال، عن ابن بكير عن زرارة بن أعين عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: إن ملكا عظيم الشأن كان في مجلس له فتناول الرب تبارك وتعالى ففقد فما يدري أين هو
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/374] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara b. A’yan from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: There was a king of great status who was in a council of his when he touched upon (started discussing) the Lord Blessed and Elevated. Suddenly he disappeared and it was not known where he was (could not be found).

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 5. [5/375] al-Kafi: Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan b. Yahya from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: The Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saying: Relate for us the geneology of your Lord. So he (the prophet) remained three days not answering them then it was revealed “Say: He is Allah the One” to its end (112:1-4).
records.arabicText: [375/5] الكافي: أحمد بن إدريس، عن محمد بن عبدالجبار، عن صفوان بن يحيى، عن أبي أيوب، عن محمد بن مسلم، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: إن اليهود سألوا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله فقالوا: انسب لنا ربك فلبث ثلاثا لا يجيبهم ثم نزل قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ إلى آخرها
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5. [5/375] al-Kafi: Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan b. Yahya from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saying: Relate for us the geneology of your Lord. So he (the prophet) remained three days not answering them then it was revealed “Say: He is Allah the One” to its end (112:1-4).

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 6. [6/376] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from Muhammad al-Attar from al-Ash’ari from al-Maythami from Safwan b. Yahya from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: The Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saying: Relate for us the geneology of your Lord. So he (the prophet) remained three days not answering them, then it was revealed this chapter (Surat Tawhid) to its end. I (Muhammad) said: What is the meaning of Samad? He said: The one who does not have cavity.
records.arabicText: [376/6] التوحيد: ابن الوليد، عن محمد العطار، عن الاشعري، عن الميثمي، عن صفوان بن يحيى، عن أبي أيوب، عن محمد بن مسلم، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: إن اليهود سألوا رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله فقالوا: انسب لنا ربك فلبث ثلاثا لا يجيبهم، ثم نزلت هذه السورة إلى آخرها فقلت: ما الصمد؟ فقال: الذي ليس بمجوف
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. [6/376] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from Muhammad al-Attar from al-Ash’ari from al-Maythami from Safwan b. Yahya from Abi Ayyub from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saying: Relate for us the geneology of your Lord. So he (the prophet) remained three days not answering them, then it was revealed this chapter (Surat Tawhid) to its end. I (Muhammad) said: What is the meaning of Samad? He said: The one who does not have cavity.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 7. [7/377] al-Tawhid: My father from Sa’d from Ibn Isa from Ibn Fadhal from al-Halabi and Zurara from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: Allah Blessed and Elevated is One, Compact, He does not have any hollowness, while the spirit is a creation from among His creations and is an aid, support and power which Allah places in the hearts of the messengers and believers.
records.arabicText: [377/7] التوحيد: أبي، عن سعد، عن ابن عيسى، عن ابن فضال، عن الحلبي وزرارة، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: إن الله تبارك وتعالى أحد صمد، ليس له جوف، وإنما الروح خلق من خلقه نصر وتأييد وقوة يجعله الله في قلوب الرسل والمؤمنين
records.thaqalaynSanad: 7. [7/377] al-Tawhid: My father from Sa’d from Ibn Isa from Ibn Fadhal from al-Halabi and Zurara from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah Blessed and Elevated is One, Compact, He does not have any hollowness, while the spirit is a creation from among His creations and is an aid, support and power which Allah places in the hearts of the messengers and believers.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 8. [8/-] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Husayn b. Sai’d from al-Nadhr b. Suwayd from A’sim b. Humayd who raised it - he said: Ali b. al-Husayn عليه السلام was asked about Tawhid (Unity of God) so he said: Allah Mighty and Majestic knew that there would in the last age peoples who delve deep (wanting to know) so Allah the Elevated brought down “Say: He is Allah One” (chapter 112) and the verses in Surat al-Hadid until His words “and He is Knowing of that within the hearts” (57:6) so whoever looks (desires) beyond that then he has perished.
records.arabicText: [-/8] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن الحسين بن سعيد، عن النضر بن سويد عن عاصم بن حميد (رفعه) (قال:) قال: سئل علي بن الحسين عليه السلام عن التوحيد فقال: إن الله عزوجل علم أنه يكون في آخر الزمان أقوام متعمقون فأنزل الله تعالى قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ والآيات من سورة الحديد إلى قوله: وَهُوَ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ فمن رام وراء ذلك فقد هلك
records.thaqalaynSanad: 8. [8/-] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Husayn b. Sai’d from al-Nadhr b. Suwayd from A’sim b. Humayd who raised it - he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Ali b. al-Husayn عليه السلام was asked about Tawhid (Unity of God) so he said: Allah Mighty and Majestic knew that there would in the last age peoples who delve deep (wanting to know) so Allah the Elevated brought down “Say: He is Allah One” (chapter 112) and the verses in Surat al-Hadid until His words “and He is Knowing of that within the hearts” (57:6) so whoever looks (desires) beyond that then he has perished.

records.chapter: Prohibition against Speculating and Speaking about God’s Nature
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 9. [9/378] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from al-Yaqtini from Ibn Abi Najran who said: I asked Aba Ja’far the Second عليه السلام about Tawhid saying: Should I imagine a thing (when conceiving of God)? He said: Yes, (something) neither cognizable nor delimited. Whatever your imagination falls upon is different than He. Nothing is like Him and imaginations do not reach Him. How can imaginations reach Him when He is other than what can be cognized and other than what is represented (images formed) in the imaginations? It is imagined something neither cognizable nor delimited.
records.arabicText: [378/9] التوحيد: ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن اليقطيني، عن ابن أبي نجران قال: سألت أبا جعفر الثاني عليه السلام عن التوحيد فقلت: أتوهم شيئا؟ فقال: نعم غير معقول ولا محدود، فما وقع وهمك عليه من شئ فهو خلافه، لا يشبهه شئ ولا تدركه الاوهام، كيف تدركه الاوهام وهو خلاف ما يعقل وخلاف ما يتصور في الاوهام؟ إنما يتوهم شئ غير معقول ولا محدود
records.thaqalaynSanad: 9. [9/378] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from al-Yaqtini from Ibn Abi Najran who said: I asked Aba Ja’far the Second عليه السلام about Tawhid saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Should I imagine a thing (when conceiving of God)? He said: Yes, (something) neither cognizable nor delimited. Whatever your imagination falls upon is different than He. Nothing is like Him and imaginations do not reach Him. How can imaginations reach Him when He is other than what can be cognized and other than what is represented (images formed) in the imaginations? It is imagined something neither cognizable nor delimited.

records.chapter: Innate Knowledge and the World of Particles
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/379] Ma’ani al-Akhbar: My father from Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Ibn Udhayna from Zurara who said: I asked Aba Ja’far عليه السلام about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “Hunafa for Allah, not associating with Him (anything)” (22:31). I said: What is the Hanifiyya? He said: The Fitra.
records.arabicText: [379/1] معاني الاخبار: أبي، عن علي، عن أبيه، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن ابن اذينة، عن زرارة قال: سألت أبا جعفر عليه السلام عن قول الله عزوجل حُنَفَاءَ لِلَّهِ غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِ فقلت: ما الحنيفية؟ قال: هي الفطرة
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/379] Ma’ani al-Akhbar: My father from Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr from Ibn Udhayna from Zurara
records.thaqalaynMatn: who said: I asked Aba Ja’far عليه السلام about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “Hunafa for Allah, not associating with Him (anything)” (22:31). I said: What is the Hanifiyya? He said: The Fitra.

records.chapter: Innate Knowledge and the World of Particles
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/380] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Hashim from Ibn Abi Umayr from Hisham b. Salim from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام. He (Hisham) said: I said: “The Fitra of Allah which He created the people upon” (30:30)? He (the Imam) said: (that is) Tawhid.
records.arabicText: [380/2] التوحيد: ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن ابن هاشم، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن هشام بن سالم، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: قلت: فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا قال: التوحيد

records.chapter: Innate Knowledge and the World of Particles
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/381] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Hashim and Ibn Yazid from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “The Fitra of Allah which He created the people upon” (30:30). He said: He created them upon Tawhid.
records.arabicText: [381/3] التوحيد: ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن ابن هاشم وابن يزيد معا، عن ابن فضال، عن ابن بكير، عن زرارة، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام في قول الله عزوجل فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا قال: فطرهم على التوحيد
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/381] al-Tawhid: Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Hashim and Ibn Yazid from Ibn Fadhal from Ibn Bukayr from Zurara from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام about the words of Allah Mighty and Majestic: “The Fitra of Allah which He created the people upon” (30:30).
records.thaqalaynMatn: He said: He created them upon Tawhid.

records.chapter: Innate Knowledge and the World of Particles
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/-] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abi Nasr from Aban b. Uthman from Ismail al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said: The Sharia (Law-code) of Nuhعليه السلام  was that Allah be worshipped by Tawhid and Ikhlas (sincerity) and to shun the idols - and it is the Fitra which He created the people upon …
records.arabicText: [-/4] الكافي: على بن إبراهيم، عن أبيه، عن أحمد بن محمد بن أبي نصر، عن أبان بن عثمان، عن إسماعيل الجعفي، عن أبى جعفر عليه السلام قال: كانت شريعة نوح عليه السلام أن يعبد الله بالتوحيد والإخلاص وخلع الأنداد وهي الفطرة التي فطر الناس عليها ...
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/-] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abi Nasr from Aban b. Uthman from Ismail al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Sharia (Law-code) of Nuhعليه السلام  was that Allah be worshipped by Tawhid and Ikhlas (sincerity) and to shun the idols - and it is the Fitra which He created the people upon …
